summary: date-fns-lite performs install-time host/VM reconnaissance and exfiltration to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: date-fns-lite@1.0.9 advertises itself as a lightweight date formatting utility but ships a postinstall.js that runs unconditionally on `npm install`. The script enumerates extensive host and virtualization details (kernel, cgroups, dmesg, CPU flags, devices, KVM/virtio/vsock, capabilities, network interfaces and routes, DMI/SMBIOS, seccomp) and executes container/Firecracker VM escape probes, including sudo-prefixed operations such as `nsenter --target 1 --net`, mounting host `/proc` via `/proc/1/root/proc`, and reading `/proc/1/root/*`. All collected output is aggregated into `/tmp/firecracker_escape_report.json` and POSTed via `curl` to `http://115.190.124.243:9082/escape`, a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint with no relationship to any date library or legitimate publisher. The package name mimics the popular `date-fns` library, making this a typosquat carrying an install-time reconnaissance and exfiltration payload; where passwordless sudo is available the privileged probes may also produce host-level effects on the installer's machine or its container/VM host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
